children.heading: Articles
children.label: 1 Articles
children.text: The term “articles” means articles of incorporation for an incorporated body or the functional equivalent or other similar documents specified by the Secretary for other business entities.

children.heading: Developmental capital
children.label: 2 Developmental capital
children.text: The term “developmental capital” means capital in the form of equity capital investments in rural business investment companies with an objective of fostering economic development in rural areas.

children.heading: Employee welfare benefit plan; pension plan
children.label: 3 Employee welfare benefit plan; pension plan

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The terms “employee welfare benefit plan” and “pension plan” have the meanings given the terms in .section 1002 of title 29

children.label: i
children.text: public and private pension or retirement plans subject to this subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: similar plans not covered by this subchapter that have been established, and that are maintained, by the Federal Government or any State (including by a political subdivision, agency, or instrumentality of the Federal Government or a State) for the benefit of employees.

children.label: A
children.text: common or preferred stock or a similar instrument, including subordinated debt with equity features; and

children.label: B
children.text: any other type of equity-like financing that might be necessary to facilitate the purposes of this Act, excluding financing such as senior debt or other types of financing that competes with routine loanmaking of commercial lenders.

children.label: A
children.text: debentures purchased or guaranteed by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: participating securities purchased or guaranteed by the Secretary; and

children.label: C
children.text: preferred securities outstanding as of .May 13, 2002

children.heading: License
children.label: 6 License
children.text: The term “license” means a license issued by the Secretary as provided in .section 2009cc–3(e) of this title

children.heading: Limited liability company
children.label: 7 Limited liability company
children.text: The term “limited liability company” means a business entity that is organized and operating in accordance with a State limited liability company law approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Member
children.label: 8 Member
children.text: The term “member” means, with respect to a rural business investment company that is a limited liability company, a holder of an ownership interest or a person otherwise admitted to membership in the limited liability company.

children.heading: Operational assistance
children.label: 9 Operational assistance
children.text: The term “operational assistance” means management, marketing, and other technical assistance that assists a rural business concern with business development.

children.heading: Participation agreement
children.label: 10 Participation agreement
children.text: The term “participation agreement” means an agreement, between the Secretary and a rural business investment company granted final approval under , that requires the rural business investment company to make investments in smaller enterprises in rural areas.section 2009cc–3(e) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the paid-in capital and paid-in surplus of a corporate rural business investment company;

children.label: II
children.text: the contributed capital of the partners of a partnership rural business investment company; or

children.label: III
children.text: the equity investment of the members of a limited liability company rural business investment company; and

children.label: I
children.text: unfunded commitments may be counted as private capital for purposes of approval by the Secretary of any request for leverage; but

children.label: II
children.text: leverage shall not be funded based on the commitments.

children.label: i
children.text: any funds borrowed by a rural business investment company from any source;

children.label: ii
children.text: any funds obtained through the issuance of leverage; or

children.label: I
children.text: funds obtained from the business revenues (excluding any governmental appropriation) of any federally chartered or government-sponsored enterprise established prior to ;May 13, 2002

children.label: II
children.text: funds invested by an employee welfare benefit plan or pension plan; and

children.label: III
children.text: any qualified nonprivate funds (if the investors of the qualified nonprivate funds do not control, directly or indirectly, the management, board of directors, general partners, or members of the rural business investment company).

children.label: A
children.text: funds directly or indirectly invested in any applicant or rural business investment company on or before , by any Federal agency, other than the Department of Agriculture, under a provision of law explicitly mandating the inclusion of those funds in the definition of the term “private capital”; andMay 13, 2002

children.label: B
children.text: funds invested in any applicant or rural business investment company by 1 or more entities of any State (including by a political subdivision, agency, or instrumentality of the State and including any guarantee extended by those entities) in an aggregate amount that does not exceed 33 percent of the private capital of the applicant or rural business investment company.

children.label: A
children.text: a public, private, or cooperative for-profit or nonprofit organization;

children.label: B
children.text: a for-profit or nonprofit business controlled by an Indian tribe on a Federal or State reservation or other federally recognized Indian tribal group; or

children.label: C
children.text: any other person or entity;

children.label: A
children.text: has been granted final approval by the Secretary under ; andsection 2009cc–3(e) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: has entered into a participation agreement with the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: a net financial worth of not more than $6,000,000, as of the date on which assistance is provided under this subchapter to the rural business concern; and

children.label: I
children.text: if the rural business concern is not required by law to pay State (and local, if any) income taxes at the enterprise level, the net income (determined without regard to this clause), multiplied by the marginal State income tax rate (or by the combined State and local income tax rates, as applicable) that would have applied if the business concern were a corporation; and

children.label: II
children.text: the net income (so determined) less any deduction for State (and local) income taxes calculated under subclause (I), multiplied by the marginal Federal income tax rate that would have applied if the rural business concern were a corporation; or

children.label: B
children.text: satisfies the standard industrial classification size standards established by the Administrator of the Small Business Administration for the industry in which the rural business concern is primarily engaged.
